name: EGFR-TM-001
rationale: Extends the pyrazolo-indazole scaffold with a trifluoromethyl group that fills the enlarged Met790M hydrophobic pocket while morpholine provides solubility.
expected_advantages: CF3 group exploits T790M enlarged gatekeeper pocket
Morpholine at solvent-exposed front pocket aids aqueous solubility
Amide NH and ring N anchor to Met793 hinge via H-bonds
No covalent warhead — reversible binder for safety

name: EGFR-TM-002
rationale: Pyrido[2,3-d]pyrimidine scaffold with 2-amino substituent bearing a CF3-phenyl to probe the Met790M pocket and acetylpiperazine for solubilization.
expected_advantages: Pyrido-pyrimidine hinge binder distinct from quinazoline
CF3-aniline fills bulky T790M hydrophobic cavity
Acetylpiperazine reduces hERG liability vs plain piperazine
MW ~430, LogP ~2.8 — oral-druggable profile

name: EGFR-TM-003
rationale: 2-Aryl-3H-quinoxalin-2-one scaffold where the lactam NH donates to Met793 hinge and the fluoroaniline amide explores a deep hydrophobic sub-pocket.
expected_advantages: Quinoxalinone scaffold avoids quinazoline-4-amine class
Fluoroaniline creates selectivity over WT Thr790
Pyridine carboxamide points toward DFG Asp855
TPSA ~90 Å² — good oral absorption predicted

name: EGFR-TM-006
rationale: Pyrimido[4,5-b]indole scaffold presenting a CF3-fluoroaniline toward the gatekeeper pocket and methylpiperazine for front-pocket solubilization.
expected_advantages: Fused tricyclic provides rigid geometry for selectivity
2,3-disubstituted aniline with CF3+F fills Met790M uniquely
Methylpiperazine tunable for PK and reduces P-gp efflux
NH-pyrimidine donates H-bond to Met793 carbonyl

name: EGFR-TM-010
rationale: N-aryl pyrazolo[3,4-b]pyridine scaffold with amide linkage to CF3-phenyl targeting Met790M and morpholine-pyridine at C5 for front-pocket engagement.
expected_advantages: Pyrazole methyl + N-aryl combo fills gatekeeper region selectively
CF3-benzamide provides strong hydrophobic interaction with T790M
Morpholinyl pyridine side chain solubilizes and reduces clearance
NH amide H-bonds to DFG-region Asp855

name: EGFR-TM-012
rationale: 2-Amino-6-aryl naphthyridine bearing fluorophenyl-pyridyl biaryl to occupy the extended hydrophobic groove of T790M and methoxycarbonylpiperazine for solubility.
expected_advantages: Biaryl fluorophenyl-pyridyl exploits T790M enlarged pocket
Naphthyridine N1/N5 provide complementary hinge contacts
Carbamate-piperazine reduces basicity avoiding hERG
Restricted rotation of biaryl selects for active conformation

name: EGFR-TM-013
rationale: N-methyl benzimidazole bearing CF3-phenyl at C2 to fill the Met790M pocket and a pyridine carboxamide that reaches Asp855 in DFG-in conformation.
expected_advantages: Benzimidazole is a well-tolerated drug-like hinge scaffold
CF3-phenyl at C2 creates T790M selectivity over Thr790 WT
Pyridine amide NH reaches DFG Asp855 — dual anchor
N-methyl eliminates NH metabolic liability

name: EGFR-TM-014
rationale: Isoquinoline scaffold with pyridine amide pointing toward Asp855 and a fluorotoluene amine anchoring the gatekeeper pocket of T790M.
expected_advantages: Isoquinoline N acts as H-bond acceptor at hinge Met793
Fluorotoluene aniline selectively disfavors WT smaller Thr790
Nicotinamide extends toward DFG providing additional binding energy
Morpholine at C6 keeps TPSA ~100 Å² for oral dosing

name: EGFR-TM-015
rationale: Imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine fused bicycle where imidazole N donates to Met793 hinge and a CF3-aniline amide occupies the deep hydrophobic T790M gatekeeper cavity.
expected_advantages: Imidazo[1,2-a]pyridine is a novel hinge scaffold vs all prior art
CF3-aniline fits bulky Met790M but clashes with WT Thr790
Direct amide NH → Asp855 H-bond in DFG-in conformation
MW ~400, LogP ~2.5, TPSA ~80 Å² — excellent oral profile
